macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Position on Saris Adam and the Status of One Who Was Once Fertile

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Rabbi Akiva's ruling on saris adam
The problem of issur lav and mamzer status for offspring
Rav Yosef's solution: the tanna of the mishnah is from Rabbi Akiva's school
Distinguishing between issur lav that creates mamzer and those that don't

macro_segments.title: Rava's Argument: Practical Infertility and the Big Picture Approach

micro_segments.title: Rava's challenge: how can there be a mitzvah of yibum if the person cannot produce children
Rava's proof from a person dying: moments before death one is infertile yet yibum applies
Rebutting Rabbi Eliezer's objection to Rava's proof
The distinction between temporary inability and actual broken equipment

macro_segments.title: Identification and Definition of Saris Chama

micro_segments.title: What is saris chama: someone never fertile for even one moment
Physical indicators of saris chama: the urination test and other signs
Causes of saris chama: maternal heat exposure and intoxicating drink during pregnancy
Rav Ami's teaching about one smitten from the womb
Concern for recovery: the halakhic presumption that a systemic condition persists
The bechor animal three-checks precedent and distinguishing one limb from whole body

macro_segments.title: Distinguishing Saris Chama from Saris Adam and the Challenge to Rabbi Eliezer

micro_segments.title: The case of a twenty-year-old with no pubic hairs as identifying a saris
The mishnah's ruling that saris does not do chalitzah or yibum
Rabbi Eliezer's contradiction: he says saris chama does chalitzah
Debate over whether Rabbi Eliezer retracted his position
Rabbi Elazar's counter: Rabbi Eliezer did not retract; the braita is for punishment purposes only

macro_segments.title: Physical Maturity, Adult Status, and the Age of Twenty

micro_segments.title: The case of eating forbidden fat from age twelve to eighteen without pubic hair development
Rav's position: retroactive adult status from twelve when saris is identified at eighteen
Shmuel's position: adult status only from age eighteen/twenty when identified
The case where pubic hair appears after age eighteen: does this change the saris status
Rav Yosef's challenge to Rav using the case of an ailonit and knas
Abaye's response: no na'arus period for ailonit; she jumps from ketanah to bogeres

macro_segments.title: Identifying the Physical Characteristics of Saris Chama and Ailonit

micro_segments.title: Overview: saris chama, ailonit, and ben shemonah all require waiting until age twenty
The problem of ben shemonah: how can an eighth-month baby survive to age twenty
The solution: eighth-month babies that survive are actually held-over seventh-month babies
Rava's practical ruling: a woman whose husband traveled for twelve months can have a kosher child
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's rule: thirty-day survival removes nefel status

macro_segments.title: The Signs and Characteristics of Saris Chama

micro_segments.title: Age twenty without pubic hair is the primary indicator; later hair does not change status
Secondary physical indicators: smooth skin, soft/affected hair
Multiple traditions on identifying saris: urination without arc, watery semen, lack of heat
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar: affected voice indistinguishable from female voice

macro_segments.title: The Debate Over Whether All or One Sign of Saris is Required

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's position: all simanim must be present for saris status
Rabbi Yochanan's position: even one siman suffices for saris status
Clarification: when pubic or facial hair is present, all simanim are needed
The case of Rav Nachman: checking for one siman to disprove saris status
Conclusion: the actual case likely involved some facial/pubic hair

macro_segments.title: Saris and Ailonit Cannot Perform or Receive Meaningful Chalitzah or Yibum

micro_segments.title: The final mishnah rule: saris does not do chalitzah or yibum
Textual inference: saris here means saris chama because paired with ailonit
Rabbi Akiva's position confirmed: natural saris does not do chalitzah or yibum
If saris does yibum it invalidates the woman; chalitzah does not invalidate
Same ruling applies to ailonit: yibum invalidates, chalitzah does not
Ruling assumes ailonit is not inherently a zona; she remains permitted to kohanim
